New GLM5 Turbo update is insane. So, China just dropped a brand new AI model called GLM5 Turbo. And this thing is not like anything you've seen before. This is not a chatbot. This is not some writing tool. This is an AI that actually does stuff for you like it opens your browser, runs your code, sends your emails all by itself. And I am going to break down exactly how it works, why it matters, and how you can use it right now. Let us get into it. So, first things first, what even is GLM5 Turbo? Because if you have not heard of it yet, you are about to hear about it everywhere. A Chinese AI company called GPU AI just built one of the biggest AI models on the planet. I am talking 744 billion parameters. That is massive. And then they said cool now let us make a faster version of it. That is GLM5 Turbo. It is the speed version, the one built specifically for AI agents. And that is the part that is going to blow your mind. But before I explain why, let me tell you what makes the base model so wild. Because the base model alone is already crazy. GM5 uses something called a mixture of experts architecture. Now, I know that sounds fancy, but think of it like this. Imagine you have a team of 256 specialists. Every time you ask a question, only the best eight specialists jump in to answer it. The rest sit down. So, you get the brain power of a huge team, but you only use a tiny part of it each time. That is how GLM5 works. It has 744 billion parameters total, but only about 40 billion activate for each task. That means it is powerful, but it does not waste energy. Smart, right? Now, here is where it gets even crazier. This model was trained on 28.5 trillion tokens of data. That is a lot of internet. And it has a 200,000 token context window. That means you can feed it an entire book or a massive codebase or a 500page document and it will read the whole thing and understand it. Most models cannot do that. This one can. And get this, it is open- source MIT license. That means anyone can use it. Anyone can build on it. Anyone can plug it into their own systems for free. That alone is huge. Now, let us talk about the turbo part because that is the real update. See, the base GLM5 model is powerful, but some developers said it was a bit slow, too heavy for real-time tasks. Not great for running AI agents that need to move fast. So, Jepu AI built GLM5 Turbo. And Turbo is built from the ground up for one thing, speed. Specifically, speed for AI agents. What does that mean? It means this model is designed to work inside systems like OpenClaw where an AI agent can open a browser, click buttons, fill out forms, search the web, write reports, send messages all on its own without you touching anything. That is what GLM5 Turbo is built for. Not chatting, doing. And that is a big deal because right now most AI models are built for conversations. You type something, it types back. Cool. But GLM5 Turbo is built for action. It is built for tool calling, for browser control, for running commands in a terminal, for solving problems step by step without you holding its hand. Think about what that means for your business. Think about what that means for your workflows. You could have an AI agent that researches your competitors, gathers data, writes a full report, and puts it in a Google doc all while you are eating lunch. That is the kind of thing GLM5 Turbo is made for. Hey, if we have not met already, I am the digital avatar of Julian Goldie, CEO of Goldie Agency. Whilst he is helping clients get more leads and customers, I am here to help you get the latest AI updates. Julian Goldie reads every single comment. So, make sure you drop a comment below and let us know what you think about this model. Now, let me talk about the benchmarks because numbers matter. GLM5 scored 77.8% on swbench. That is a coding benchmark where AI models have to solve real GitHub issues, real bugs, real code. And it scored close to the top proprietary models out there. We are talking GPT level, Claude level, Gemini level, but this one is open source. That is wild. It also scored a 50 on the artificial analysis intelligence index, which puts it right up there with the best models in the world. And remember, this is a model that anyone can use, anyone can run, anyone can build on top of. That changes the game. Now, here is a detail that blew my mind, and I think it will blow yours, too. This entire model was trained on 100,000 Huawei Ascent chips. Not a single Nvidia GPU. Let me say that again. Not one Nvidia chip. This is a Frontier AI model, one of the biggest in the world, trained entirely on Chinese hardware. That is a massive moment because up until now, everyone assumed you needed Nvidia to train these kinds of models. GLM5 just proved you do not. And that has huge implications for the entire AI industry. Now let us talk about what you can actually do with GLM5 Turbo because that is what matters, right? Use case number one, AI agents. You can build an agent that opens Chrome, logs into your apps, posts content, sends emails, updates spreadsheets, all automatically, all powered by GLM5 Turbo. That is not science fiction. That is happening right now. Use case number two, autonomous coding. You can point an AI agent at a code repository. It reads the code, finds the bugs, fixes them, commits the changes, runs the tests all by itself. If you are a developer, this is going to save you hours every single week. Use case number three, enterprise workflows, report generation, CRM updates, research pipelines, all the boring stuff that eats up your day. GLM5 Turbo can handle it. Use case number four, developer co-pilots, code completion, debugging, architecture, planning. This model has a 200,000 token context window. So, it can look at your entire project and understand how everything fits together. That is insane for building software. And now, if you are watching this and thinking, okay, this is cool, but how do I actually use AI like this to grow my business? Now, let me talk about something important, the community reaction, because not everyone is saying this model is perfect. And I want to be honest with you, some early users on Reddit said the quality can still vary. Some tasks it crushes, other tasks it stumbles a bit. And that is fair. This is a new model. It is going to get better. But the fact that it is open source means thousands of developers are already working on improving it. That is the power of open source. The community makes it better every single day. But here is the thing that most people are missing. The real story is not just about JLM5 Turbo. The real story is about where AI is going. We are moving from AI that talks to AI that acts, from chat bots to agents, from typing prompts to setting up autonomous systems that run your business while you sleep. And GLM5 Turbo is one of the first models built specifically for that future. Think about it. A 744 billion parameter model, open source, 200,000 token contexts optimized for agents trained without any NVIDIA hardware. That is five massive things in one release. And most people have not even heard about it yet. But you have because you are watching this video and that puts you ahead. Now, let me give you a quick comparison so you can see where this fits. Most top models right now have a context window of about 128,000 tokens. GLM5 has 200,000. That is a big jump. And for agent tasks, that extra context matters a lot because agents need to remember what they have done. They need to hold information across long workflows. A bigger context window means smarter agents that do not forget what they are doing halfway through a task. And the mixture of experts architecture means it runs efficiently. You are not burning through compute for nothing. Only the parts of the model that matter for your specific task light up. Everything else stays quiet. That is elegant engineering. Now, if I were you, here is what I would do next. I would go try GLM5 Turbo. I would set up an agent in OpenClaw. I would give it a real task. something you do every week that takes you an hour and see if GLM5 Turbo can do it in five minutes because that is the test, not benchmarks, not paper scores. Can it actually save you time? And based on everything I have seen, the answer is yes. This is one of the first models designed specifically for AI agents instead of chat bots. And that shift is going to change everything. Mark my words now real quick.
